mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession risk, prompting equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 100 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in less than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the median 5-day return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized pullback in household balance sheets and spending capacity, which mechanically reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in at least two distinct recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in the industrial production index reflects robust capacity utilization and healthy supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day Spearman correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and SPX forward returns drops below 0.10 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings per share through higher volume and pricing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions flow through to equity prices.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show negative correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold, the cost of capital rises sharply and liquidity conditions tighten, forcing equity risk premiums higher and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where the rate crosses the 4.0% threshold, or the 60-day cumulative return spread between high-rate and low-rate regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls creates a reinforcing negative feedback loop in consumer confidence and spending, which accelerates earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of historical labor market downturns, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit outstanding alongside stable housing starts indicates sustained household borrowing and consumption demand, which supports top-line revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in less than 45% of observations, or the cross-correlation between credit growth and equity returns turns negative during peak inflation periods.
